assumptions: The developer can use a browser and accept Railway Terms of Service.
No Railway account or project exists yet.
A GitHub account is available if the GitHub deploy candidate is selected.

developer_goal: Create a Railway account, deploy a first web application, and observe a running deployment.

first_success.outcome: Railway has built and started the selected service and the initial deployment is ready on the Project Canvas.
first_success.observable_signal: The deployment reaches Active / ready state after the initial deploy completes.

route_selection.rule: Quick Start presents GitHub, CLI, Docker image, and template as peer deploy options; lexicographic winner is unresolved while signup fields remain unverified.

candidates.name: Dashboard deploy from GitHub (NextJS sample fork)
candidates.reason: Recommended first-code path in Quick Start, but signup and verification order are not public.

candidates.name: Deploy a template
candidates.reason: Peer Quick Start path that may avoid forking sample code but still needs account and any template variables.

candidates.name: CLI railway init + railway up
candidates.reason: Different interaction surface from the Dashboard-first account journey unless CLI is chosen as the goal.

candidates.name: Docker image service
candidates.reason: Peer Dashboard path with empty project plus image name.

required_path.action: Sign up or log in at Railway and accept Terms for a new account.
required_path.observable_result: The developer reaches an authenticated Railway dashboard or trial state.

required_path.action: Create a New Project and choose the selected deploy source (GitHub repo, template, or Docker image).
required_path.observable_result: Railway opens the project creation flow for that source.

required_path.action: If using GitHub, link and authorize the GitHub account and select the repository to deploy.

required_fields.label: GitHub account link and repository access

required_path.observable_result: The repository is available to Railway for Deploy Now.

required_path.action: Choose Deploy Now (or Deploy for non-GitHub peers) to start the initial deployment.
required_path.observable_result: Railway creates the project and kicks off the first build and deploy.

required_path.action: On the Project Canvas, confirm the initial deployment completed and is ready.
required_path.observable_result: The deployment view shows the service ready/Active after the initial deploy.

prerequisites.description: One Railway account per person; Free Trial provides a one-time credit grant.
GitHub-linked path needs a fork or accessible repo such as the Quick Start NextJS sample.

external_gates.description: GitHub authorization is required for the GitHub deploy option.
Trial verification based on GitHub account age and activity can limit features until verify completes.

unavoidable_waits.description: Railway must queue, build, and start the container before the initial deployment is ready.

platform_outcomes.description: Deploy Now creates the project and starts the first deployment.
A successful initial deployment becomes ready/Active on the Project Canvas.

excluded.reason: Documentation is evidence, not a product action.

excluded.item: Add variables path instead of Deploy Now
excluded.reason: Quick Start directs Deploy Now for brevity.

excluded.item: Generating a public domain after Active
excluded.reason: Quick Start marks the app ready when the initial deployment completes; domain may be extra for browser output.

uncertainties.question: What is the current browser signup, identity choice, Terms, and verification order for a new Railway user?
uncertainties.impact: Account-segment action and field counts cannot be verified from public docs alone.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Fresh-account signup walkthrough with dated screenshots of Login and post-signup verification screens.

uncertainties.question: Among GitHub sample, template, and Docker image Dashboard peers, which is the lexicographic shortest route to first success?
uncertainties.impact: External gates and required fields differ; template may avoid forking while GitHub is the documented first-code recommendation.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Human judgment applying SELECTION-POLICY to the Quick Start peer options with a fixed first-success definition (Active vs public URL).
